D9 BiologyL3 ApplyReference

Beetroot Betacyanin — A Different Red Pigment Class

betacyanin (the red of beetroot, prickly pear, bougainvillea) is not anthocyanin — it's a betalain, nitrogen-containing, found only in Caryophyllales; absorbs at 537 nm

Betacyanin (betanin, λ_max ~537 nm) is a completely different chemistry from anthocyanin — it contains nitrogen instead of being a polyphenol. Found only in the order Caryophyllales (beet, spinach, prickly pear, bougainvillea). pH-stable from 3 to 7. Same red colour by very different molecular machinery.

Drag concentration; beetroot juice darkens. Toggle pH (3–9) to see stability.
